bulletin.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and gliding snow require caution. In particular steep sunny slopes: Further increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on very steep slopes medium-sized natural avalanches must be expected. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size.
Mostly the dry avalanches are medium-sized and can be released by a single winter sport participant. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection. Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. Caution is to be exercised in particular in little used terrain, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.
The no longer entirely fresh wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation wet avalanches are to be expected. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes, and at the base of rock walls.
The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist. The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. The danger of wet avalanches will increase significantly during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also on steep sunny slopes above approximately 2400 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular in little used terrain, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

The no longer entirely fresh wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2600 m.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation wet avalanches are to be expected. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes, and at the base of rock walls. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies especially on steep sunny slopes below approximately 2400 m.

tendency.highlights:
Steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
Steep sunny slopes intermediate and high altitudes: The danger of wet avalanches will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather will be very mild. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more medium-sized to large wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, in particular in steep rocky terrain, as well as on cut and grassy slopes. Snow slides on road cut slopes.
Backcountry tours should be concluded early.

In addition the no longer entirely fresh wind slabs must be taken into account. They are in some cases prone to triggering. This applies in particular on very steep shady slopes at intermediate and high altitudes. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In particular here the avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions fostered a settling of the near-surface layers. These conditions fostered a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations in particular on sunny slopes. In its middle, the snowpack is unfavourably layered.

The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m.

Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. There are large frames on the crest.
In these regions at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
Numerous medium-sized to large moist avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be warm. The danger of natural wet avalanches will not decrease for the time being.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The weather will be very mild. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

With the freezing level at 3000 metres, combined with solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.

In addition the no longer entirely fresh wind slabs must be taken into account. They are in some cases prone to triggering. This applies in particular on shady slopes. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. The avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m.

The weather conditions fostered a settling of the near-surface layers. These conditions fostered a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations in particular on sunny slopes. In its middle, the snowpack is unfavourably layered.

Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Numerous small to medium-sized moist and wet avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be warm. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will not decrease for the time being.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. The danger of wet avalanches will increase significantly during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also on steep sunny slopes above approximately 2400 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular in little used terrain, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be a rapid increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes, and at the base of rock walls. The runout zones are to be treated with caution. Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.
The no longer entirely fresh wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep north, east and west facing slopes.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2400 m.

tendency.highlights:
Steep shady slopes high altitudes: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
Steep sunny slopes intermediate and high altitudes: The danger of wet avalanches will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be sunny. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In particular below approximately 2500 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The weather conditions fostered a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized and can mostly still be released by large loads. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In these regions less snow than usual is lying.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The weather conditions will facilitate a substantial strengthening of the near-surface layers during the night. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The spring-like weather conditions in the late morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: At low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack. Places where surface hoar has been covered with snow are precarious. Weak layers in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. In addition the wind slabs of recent weeks are capable of being triggered still.
Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized and can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible from the late morning, even large ones in isolated cases. In particular on steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.

The older wind slabs can be released in isolated cases at intermediate and high altitudes. These can in some places be released by people and reach medium size.

Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise in the late morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a significant increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.
avalancheActivity.comment:
On very steep sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place large natural avalanches, capable of reaching a long way in some cases, must be expected in isolated cases.

Steep slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The older wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads,. Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m.

Isolated medium-sized natural moist avalanches are possible in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent barely recognisable for beginners.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment: The dangerous spots are found on steep slopes, especially grassy ones, as well as in areas near the ridges and at the base of rock walls, also due to the possibility of debris falling from above. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on sunny slopes. Gliding avalanches are also to be expected. The wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering. Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment: The weather conditions will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny. Continuous warming.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Caution is to be exercised in areas with glide cracks. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of gliding avalanches being released will increase quickly in particular on steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes. Gliding avalanches are possible at any time, even large ones in isolated cases. They can also be released in the morning. In addition on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes, very occasional medium-sized slab avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The wind slabs can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
And in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases. The danger of wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation individual small and medium-sized wet avalanches are to be expected.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases. The danger of wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation individual small and medium-sized wet avalanches are to be expected.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack will consolidate.
